---
title: "Military locations"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Python section of code

Downloads packages in terminal

```{bash}
pip install pandas geopandas thefuzz rapidfuzz fiona shapely

```

Loads in csv file

```{python}
#importing pandas to use 
import pandas as pd

#loads in csv file 
dod = pd.read_csv("pfas_progress_june_2021.csv")


```

cleaning data

```{python}
import re

def clean_name(x):
  x = str(x).lower() #puts everything in lowercase 
  x = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9 ]', '',x) # removes eveything but letters, numbers, and spaces 
  x = re.sub(r'\b(ft|fort)\b', 'fort' , x) #standardizes several names to fort
  x = re.sub(r'b(aaf|aaf |army airfield)\b', 'aaf', x) # turns several things to aaf
  x = re.sub(r'\b(afb|air force base)\b','afb',x) # turns several things to afb 
  x = re.sub(r'\s+','',x).strip() # fixes spacing issues
  return x

#making a new column called clean that cleans the installation_Name column 
dod["clean"] = dod["Installation_Name"].apply(clean_name)
dod.head()
  
```

importing military shapefile datasets ( need shapefiles)

```{python}
#import geopandas as gpd
#loads in the files 
mirta = pd.read_csv("/Users/masonchandler/Downloads/mirta_-223606765265040761.csv")
#tiger = gpd.read_file()
#fuds = gpd.read_file()
#bts = gpd.read_file()
```

```{python}


# Apply the cleaning function to the MIRTA dataframe
mirta["clean"] = mirta["Site Name"].apply(clean_name)



```

Matching sites

```{python}
from rapidfuzz import process

def get_best_match(name,choices):
  match = process.extractOne(name,choices,score_cutoff = 80)
  if match:
    return match[0], match[1]
  else:
    return None, None
  
  choices = mirta["clean"].tolist()
  
  dod["matched_name"], dod["match_score"] = zip(*dod["clean"].apply(lambda x: get_best_match(x,choices)))
```

merging

```{python}
merged = dod.merge(mirta, left_on="matched_name", right_on="clean", how="left", suffixes = ("_dod", "_mirta"))

```
